title: Use soft regime memberships for robust training instead of hard regime bins
summary: You already use hard regime keys and shrinkage blends.
rationale: You already use hard regime keys and shrinkage blends. PG-DRO suggests robustness improves when group membership is probabilistic rather than hard, which fits this task because samples near race-phase, tire-age, and position cutoffs are ambiguous and likely misgrouped by fixed bins.
implementation_hint: Define soft memberships over phase/tyre-age/position/compound regimes from distances to bin edges, then use those weights either in a probabilistic group DRO loss for a light model or as soft sample reweighting when fitting XGB/CatBoost experts. Compare against the current hard regime blend under the same folds.
expected_effect: Should reduce overfitting to arbitrary cut boundaries and improve worst-slice behavior under season/track drift.
risk: More moving parts in weighting can make CV noisy; if soft groups are too diffuse, the method collapses back to average-risk training.
